name: S-13 --check-first-use-of-day writes a per-day marker on first invocation and skips on subsequent ones
command: printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\nexit 0\n' > up.sh && chmod +x up.sh && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/standards/freshness-gate.sh" --check-first-use-of-day --now 2026-05-25T10:00:00Z --upstream-stub ./up.sh 2>err.txt && grep -q 'first use of day' err.txt && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/standards/freshness-gate.sh" --check-first-use-of-day --now 2026-05-25T11:00:00Z --upstream-stub ./up.sh 2>err2.txt && grep -q 'already refreshed today' err2.txt && echo dedup 1>&2
